title: NASA’s permanent Moon base plans start with three missions this year
summary: On Tuesday, NASA announced several upcoming lunar missions to the Moon's South Pole region. These missions will pave the way for the crewed Artemis landing slated for 2028, starting with three Moon Ba
content:
On Tuesday, NASA announced several upcoming lunar missions to the Moon's South Pole region. These missions will pave the way for the crewed Artemis landing slated for 2028, starting with three Moon Base missions NASA says are "the first of more than a dozen missions that will be announced this year."
Moon Base I, launching "no earlier than fall 2026," will use Blue Origin's Blue Moon Mark 1 Endurance lander to bring NASA payloads to the Moon, including the Stereo Camera for Lunar Plume-Surface Studies instrument and the Laser Retroreflective Array. NASA will use these "to study how thrusters interact with the Moon's surface" and help "orbi …

Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
summary: The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we've spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google Health,
content:
The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we've spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google Health, as previously announced, and many of the responses we've seen so far are full of confusion, frustration, and requests to get the old app back. 
One post on Reddit calls out a common issue, saying, "I can't even completely fill up my home screen. They only have 2 large tiles available and I can't just scroll down to see everything." The landing page has a small section up top showing steps and some other basic stats, but part of the app's main  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.summary: Fitbit应用已停用。随着新Fitbit Air的推出，Google已正式用Google Health取代了它。

title: GE’s nugget ice maker is nearly half off if you buy it refurbished
summary: A refurbished GE Profile Opal 2.0 Ultra Nugget Ice Maker is on sale for $264.99 at Woot (40% off the $449 regular price), with a 90-day warranty. The countertop appliance produces up to 38 pounds of nugget ice daily without plumbing, though it requires regular maintenance. Reviewers found it became a household favorite while supporting Wi-Fi control and smart home integration.
content:
GE’s nugget ice maker is simple to set up. | Photo by Jennifer Pattison Tuohy / The Verge	

If you’re looking for ways to make staying cool and hydrated easier this summer, Woot is currently selling a refurbished GE Profile Opal 2.0 Ultra Nugget Ice Maker for $264.99 through June 3rd. That’s $184 less than buying new at its full price, and comes with a 90-day warranty. If you want a brand new model, it’s also on sale for $359 ($90) at Best Buy, or $449 ($50 off) with a side tank included (Amazon, Best Buy,  GE).
GE Profile Opal 2.0 Ultra

Where to Buy:

 $449 $264.99 at Woot (Refurbished)
 $449.99 $359.99 at Best Buy
 $499 $449 at Amazon (with side tank)

If you’re unfamiliar with nugget ice, it’s the soft, chewable ice that cools drinks quickly and  doesn’t melt as fast as crushed ice. In the week she spent testing it, my colleague Jennifer Pattison Tuohy said the countertop appliance quickly became her family’s “favorite kitchen gadget,” making drinks taste better and even encouraging her kids to stay hydrated. It’s simple to use and makes ice quickly, churning out up to 38 pounds of chewable nugget ice per day. Setup is relatively simple, too, as it doesn’t require plumbing. The machine also supports Wi-Fi, allowing you to schedule ice production or control it with Amazon Alexa or Google Assistant.
While the ice maker does require a lot of maintenance, GE also added some upgrades aimed at making it slightly easier, including a scale-inhibiting water filter, a reusable air filter, and built-in alerts that let you know when it’s time to sanitize or descale. There’s also a touch display for checking status and connecting to Wi-Fi, along with a stainless steel scoop and a magnetic scoop holder.
Read our GE Profile Opal 2.0 Ultra Nugget Ice Maker review.

title: Sony’s sloppy Spider-Man universe gets even messier with Spider-Noir
summary: Sony is expanding its Spider-Man universe with Spider-Noir, despite ongoing questions about whether the character rights would be better served under Marvel Studios. The move follows critical success of the Spider-Verse films, which proved Sony could deliver compelling Spider-Man stories. The announcement adds another title to Sony's increasingly crowded superhero slate.
content:
After years of it seeming like the Spider-Man film rights might be better off in Marvel's hands alone, Into the Spider-Verse came along and proved that Sony was still capable of telling phenomenal stories featuring everyone's favorite webhead. Into the Spider-Verse's sumptuous visuals and focus on a different web-slinging New Yorker made it unlike any other Spider-Man adaptation. And it was genuinely shocking to see Sony follow the film up with a bigger, bolder, more imaginative sequel just a few years later.
Part of what made the first two Spider-Verse features so much fun to watch was the way they cleverly incorporated many of the lesser- …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: American Airlines is getting Starlink Wi-Fi
summary: American Airlines is installing SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi on more than 500 aircraft, with deployment beginning in Q1 2027 on Airbus A321XLR and A321neo models. Starlink will operate alongside existing providers Viasat and SES for redundancy and coverage. The deployment represents another major victory for SpaceX's satellite internet service in the aviation market.
content:
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA - MAY 12: American Airlines plane are takeoff and lands at San Francisco International Airport (SFO) in San Francisco, California, United States on May 12, 2026. (Photo by Tayfun Coskun/Anadolu via Getty Images) | Anadolu via Getty Images	

American Airlines is planning to install SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi in hundreds of its airplanes, the airline announced today. 
American says the deployment will start in the first quarter of 2027, and will span more than 500 aircraft, including its new A321XLR and A321neo planes from Airbus. Starlink will join Viastat and SES (previously known as Intelstat) as among the airline's Wi-Fi providers. 
American is the latest air carrier to turn to Elon Musk's Starlink for its Wi-Fi needs. Others include United Airlines, Southwest Airlines, Lufthansa Group, British Airways, Qatar Airways, Alaska Airlines, and Hawaiian Airlines. Meanwhile, Delta Ai …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 美国航空公司开始安装Starlink Wi-Fi服务

title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
summary: Human Archive, founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying Indian gig workers to collect physical training data for AI and robotics systems using camera-equipped caps and sensors. The startup capitalizes on India's large gig workforce to accelerate data collection that AI labs urgently need. The approach represents a novel outsourcing model for gathering real-world physical training data essential to robotics development.
content: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training data that AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire.

zh.title: 这家初创公司押注印度零工经济可以训练全球机器人
zh.summary: 由加州大学伯克利分校和斯坦福大学研究人员创立的Human Archive公司，正在支付印度零工用配备摄像头的帽子和传感器收集AI和机器人系统的物理训练数据。这家初创公司利用印度庞大的零工劳动力来加速AI实验室急需的数据收集。这种方法代表了一种新颖的外包模式，用于收集机器人开发所需的真实世界物理训练数据。

title: Memory V re-creates the Memorymoog without the massive headaches or price tag
summary: Arturia's Memory V is a software emulation of the legendary Memorymoog synthesizer, delivering its renowned sound without the reliability issues or six-figure vintage price. The Memorymoog was only produced from 1982-1985 and became notorious for both its iconic sound and frequent malfunctions. The emulation provides musicians access to classic analog synthesis at a fraction of the cost.
content:
The Memorymoog is legendary for being an absolutely massive-sounding synth and being incredibly unreliable. But now you can enjoy its classic Moog sound without the headaches or the sky-high vintage price, thanks to Arturia's Memory V emulator. 
The Memorymoog was only made between 1982 and 1985, and was the last polyphonic synth made by Moog before it declared bankruptcy in 1987. People loved its sound, which was described as being six Minimoogs in a box, and the Memorymoog+ was among the first synths to adopt MIDI. It also came out right as the synth industry was shifting from analog to digital.
It was also notorious for being rushed to  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Memory V 重现 Memorymoog 合成器，无需担忧可靠性问题和高昂价格
zh.summary: Arturia 的 Memory V 是传奇 Memorymoog 合成器的软件模拟，提供其闻名的音质，但没有可靠性问题或六位数的复古价格。Memorymoog 仅在 1982-1985 年间生产，因其标志性音质和频繁故障而臭名昭著。该模拟软件为音乐家提供了以极低成本获得经典模拟合成的方式。

title: Dutch government blocks US company from acquisition, citing ‘risk to public interest’
summary: The Dutch government blocked a U.S. company's acquisition of a cloud firm hosting the country's digital ID service, citing risks to public interest. The decision reflects Europe's broader effort to reduce dependency on American technology infrastructure, particularly for sensitive government systems. The move highlights growing geopolitical tensions around data sovereignty and tech control.
content: The move to block the acquisition of the cloud company that hosts the Dutch digital ID service comes as Europe continues to reduce its reliance on U.S. technology.

zh.title: 荷兰政府阻止美国公司收购，声称存在"公共利益风险"
zh.summary: 荷兰政府阻止了一家美国公司对托管该国数字身份服务的云计算公司的收购，理由是存在公共利益风险。该决定反映了欧洲更广泛的努力，即减少对美国技术基础设施的依赖，尤其是对敏感的政府系统。此举突显了围绕数据主权和技术控制的日益加剧的地缘政治紧张局势。

title: Saving for a Switch 2 is easier with Newegg’s gift card deal
summary: Newegg is offering $200 in Nintendo eShop credit for $170, with stackable cards allowing customers to accumulate savings toward a Switch 2 purchase. Nintendo is raising the Switch 2's price by $50 to $499.99 in September, making early gift card purchases strategically valuable. The deal provides a way to offset upcoming console price increases before the hike takes effect.
content:
If you foresee a bunch of Nintendo purchases in your future, Newegg’s deal on Nintendo gift cards might just help save your wallet. The retailer is offering a deal that lets gamers get $200 worth of credit towards games, consoles, and accessories for just $170. Best of all, the gift cards can stack in your account, letting you accrue enough to buy a Nintendo Switch 2 at a sweet discount.
Nintendo eShop gift cards

Where to Buy:

 $200 $170 at Newegg ($200)
 $100 $89.99 at Costco ($100)

You don’t have to buy gift cards to reap some savings on a Switch 2, though. Nintendo is currently offering a $499.99 “Choose Your Game” console bundle that includes a discounted game (Donkey Kong Bananza, Pokémon Pokopia, or Mario Kart World). Either way you go, it’s wise to buy a console ahead of the fall because Nintendo is raising the Switch 2’s price by $50 — up to $499.99 — in September.

This case doesn’t take away from the Roam 2’s slick

zh.title: 通过 Newegg 的礼卡优惠，为 Switch 2 存钱变得更容易
zh.summary: Newegg 以 170 美元的价格提供 200 美元的任天堂 eShop 积分，可堆叠的礼卡让客户能够累积储蓄用于购买 Switch 2。任天堂将在 9 月将 Switch 2 的价格提高 50 美元至 499.99 美元，使早期礼卡购买在战略上更具价值。该优惠提供了在价格上涨生效前抵消即将到来的主机价格上升的方式。

title: Ghost hackers: the cybersecurity mystery that nobody has solved
summary: A shadowy group that stole and publicly released the NSA's most powerful hacking tools continues to shape how companies approach cybersecurity risk, years after the original breach. The incident remains largely unsolved, leaving questions about the perpetrators' identity and motivations. The tools' release fundamentally changed the threat landscape for organizations worldwide.
content: A shadowy group that stole and dumped the NSA’s most powerful hacking tools still has implications for how companies think about digital risk today.

zh.title: 幽灵黑客：无人能解的网络安全谜团
zh.summary: 一个神秘黑客组织窃取并公开发布了美国国家安全局最强大的黑客工具，多年后仍在影响企业应对网络安全风险的方式。该事件至今基本未破，令人对肇事者的身份和动机困惑不已。这些工具的泄露从根本上改变了全球组织所面临的威胁格局。

title: Iranian hackers blamed for breach of Los Angeles transit system that took weeks to recover
summary: Iranian government-backed hackers claimed responsibility for a significant breach of Los Angeles' transit system through the fake hacktivist persona "Ababil of Minab." The attack caused weeks-long recovery efforts and exposed vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure security. The incident underscores persistent threats from state-sponsored actors targeting American public systems.
content: An Israeli cybersecurity firm said Iran’s government is behind Ababil of Minab, a fake hacktivist persona that has claimed a series of data breaches after the start of the war in Iran.

zh.title: 伊朗黑客被指责入侵洛杉矶交通系统，恢复耗时数周
zh.summary: 伊朗政府支持的黑客通过虚假黑客活动家身份"米那卜的阿巴比尔"声称对洛杉矶交通系统的重大入侵事件负责。该攻击导致数周的恢复工作，并暴露了关键基础设施安全中的漏洞。这一事件凸显了国家支持的行为体对美国公共系统的持续威胁。

title: Ferrari’s first EV is not for you
summary: Ferrari's first electric vehicle, the Luce, appears primarily designed to satisfy regulatory compliance requirements rather than serve as a flagship product, with particular emphasis on the Chinese market. The Jony Ive-designed EV carries considerable expectations given the designer's prominence, raising questions about its market positioning. The decision to prioritize compliance over premium positioning suggests challenges in Ferrari's EV transition strategy.
content: The Ferrari Luce seems to be more aimed at regulatory compliance and China, putting a lot of pressure on the Jony Ive-designed EV.

zh.title: 法拉利首款电动汽车并非为你而设
zh.summary: 法拉利首款电动汽车露琦似乎主要是为了满足监管合规要求而非成为旗舰产品，特别强调了中国市场。由乔尼·艾夫设计的电动汽车承载着相当高的期望，鉴于设计师的知名度，这引发了关于其市场定位的疑问。优先考虑合规而非溢价定位的决定表明法拉利在电动汽车转型战略上面临挑战。

title: 7-Eleven data breach affects over 185,000 people’s personal data
summary: A 7-Eleven data breach exposed personal information for over 185,000 people, including names, dates of birth, addresses, and Social Security numbers. The incident represents a significant security vulnerability affecting sensitive financial and identity data.
content: The data breach included names, dates-of-birth, postal addresses, and Social Security numbers, according to a state government listing.

zh.title: 7-Eleven数据泄露影响超过185,000人的个人信息
zh.summary: 7-Eleven数据泄露事件暴露了超过185,000人的个人信息，包括姓名、出生日期、地址和社会安全号码。这起事件反映了一个重大安全漏洞，影响了敏感的财务和身份数据。

title: Nobody wants to tell me why they only listen to their own Suno slop
summary: Users of AI music generation tool Suno are increasingly listening exclusively to their own AI-generated content rather than traditional streaming platforms, with some reporting they no longer use services like Spotify. The trend raises questions about engagement with AI-generated art versus traditional music.
content:
Do you even like art? | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

There's this alarming trend in the Suno subreddit. People aren't just prompting AI songs; they're sitting around listening almost exclusively to their own slop. And in some cases, they proudly proclaim that they don't listen to music on traditional streaming platforms anymore - it's just AI all day.
"Does anyone just listen to their own music now and not even music on Spotify anymore.?"
"I definitely listen to my own music most of the time now. Why wouldn't I? It's album after album of bangers"
"Guilty as charged. It's an infectious addiction, and I love it."
"I thought I was the only one that had an addiction to suno."
"Last.f …

Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 没有人想告诉我为什么他们只听自己的Suno生成的音乐
zh.summary: AI音乐生成工具Suno的用户越来越多地只听自己生成的AI音乐，而不是使用传统流媒体平台，有些人表示他们不再使用Spotify等服务。这一趋势引发了关于AI生成艺术与传统音乐参与度的问题。

title: Driving Porsche's most powerful car—and no, it's not a 911
summary: Porsche has unveiled its most powerful vehicle, delivering 1,139 horsepower and supporting 400 kW charging speeds, making it exceptionally fast but also extremely expensive. The car represents the brand's engineering pinnacle in performance and power.
content: 1,139 horsepower, 400 kW charging, brutally fast, and brutally expensive.

zh.title: 驾驶保时捷最强大的汽车——不，它不是911
zh.summary: 保时捷推出了其最强大的车型，提供1,139马力并支持400 kW充电速度，使其异常快速但价格极其昂贵。这款车代表了该品牌在性能和动力方面的工程巅峰。

title: Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord raises $250M at $3B valuation
summary: Stord, an Amazon fulfillment competitor founded in 2015 by former Georgia Tech students, has raised $250 million at a $3 billion valuation. The funding demonstrates investor confidence in third-party logistics alternatives to Amazon's infrastructure.
content: Stord was founded in 2015 by then-college students CEO Sean Henry  and CTO Jacob Boudreau while they were still at Georgia Tech.

zh.title: Stord融资2.5亿美元，估值达30亿美元
zh.summary: 成立于2015年的亚马逊配送竞争对手Stord由前佐治亚理工学院学生创办，已融资2.5亿美元，估值30亿美元。这笔融资表明投资者对亚马逊基础设施第三方物流替代方案充满信心。

title: AI warfare is already here
summary: International discussions on autonomous weapon systems have shifted from theoretical concerns to practical deployment scenarios, with UN Convention attendees recognizing that AI warfare technology is much closer to reality than previously expected. The realization marks a critical turning point in military technology development.
content:
The Convention on Certain Conventional Weapons, an international forum that focuses on lethal autonomous systems, is hosted twice a year at the United Nations in Geneva. When Branka Marijan attended in November 2017, she thought the five-day sessions - which dealt largely in hypotheticals, speculating on a world where warfare was fought with killer robots - would be business as usual. After all, this was technology some thought might never be developed, and likely never deployed. That year, she quickly realized, was different. That distant, imagined future was suddenly closer and realer than ever.
On the first day, some attendees watched a  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: AI战争已经来临
zh.summary: 关于自主武器系统的国际讨论已从理论关切转向实际部署场景，联合国公约参与者认识到AI战争技术比预期更接近现实。这一认识标志着军事技术发展的关键转折点。

title: Spotify is narrating magazine articles now
summary: Spotify is launching narrated versions of long-form magazine articles from major publications, with over 650 articles available to Premium subscribers alongside music and podcasts. The expansion positions Spotify as a broader content consumption platform beyond audio entertainment.
content:
Would you listen to magazine articles on Spotify? The streaming platform certainly hopes so, as it's launching a new format for narrated long-form articles, alongside its usual array of music, podcasts, and audiobooks. Starting today, more than 650 articles from publications including Rolling Stone, The Atlantic, Vogue, Variety, Billboard, Vibe, GQ, Wired, Vanity Fair, and Pitchfork will be available in English in regions where Spotify's audiobooks are available.
The narrated articles are all under two hours long, and available under the monthly audiobook allowance for Premium users. Spotify says that free users who don't pay for a subscrip …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Spotify现在朗读杂志文章
zh.summary: Spotify正在推出来自主要出版物的长篇杂志文章的朗读版本，为Premium用户提供650多篇文章，与音乐和播客一起使用。这一扩展将Spotify定位为超越音频娱乐的更广泛内容消费平台。

title: Uber president says AI spending is getting ‘harder to justify’
summary: Uber's leadership is questioning the return on AI investments after exhausting its annual budget in just four months, citing difficulty connecting spending to measurable consumer benefits. The scrutiny reflects broader tech industry concerns about whether current AI spending translates to meaningful product improvements.
content:
Uber president Andrew Macdonald (pictured) says its “hard to draw a line” between AI spending and deliverable features. | Photo: Zed Jameson/Bloomberg via Getty Images	

After reportedly exhausting its annual AI budget just four months into 2026, Uber is now questioning whether it's actually seeing meaningful returns on its investments. In an interview with Rapid Response, Uber president and chief operating officer Andrew Macdonald said the company isn't seeing a connection between rising token consumption for Claude Code and more useful features being delivered to consumers.
"That link is not there yet, right? I think maybe implicitly there is more that is getting shipped, but it's very hard to draw a line between one of those stats and, 'Okay, now we're actually producing 25 percent more useful consumer f …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Uber总裁表示AI支出变得"越来越难以证明合理性"
zh.summary: Uber领导层在仅四个月内耗尽年度预算后，质疑AI投资的回报，指出难以将支出与可衡量的消费者利益联系起来。这种审视反映了更广泛的科技行业关注，即当前AI支出是否转化为有意义的产品改进。

title: A battery-powered Starlink Mini is likely on the way
summary: Recent Starlink firmware code suggests SpaceX is developing a battery-powered version of its Starlink Mini satellite dish, enabling portable high-speed internet without external power. The feature would benefit mobile users including vanlifers and emergency responders needing reliable connectivity from remote locations.
content:
This, but no cable. | Photo by Thomas Ricker / The Verge	

Code in recent Starlink firmware suggests that SpaceX might soon release a version of its smallest internet dish with an integrated battery. A battery-powered Starlink Mini would offer untethered portability for vanlifers, emergency responders, and anyone who wants fast, low-latency internet from almost anywhere on the planet.
University researcher Jinwei Zhao spotted a number of strings hinting at the integrated battery in a May firmware release, according to PCMag. The "message DishBatteryStats" line suggests code designed to return specific fields from an integrated battery, including the current state_of_charge. If the dish were simply  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 电池供电的Starlink Mini即将推出
zh.summary: 最近的Starlink固件代码表明SpaceX正在开发其Starlink Mini卫星天线的电池供电版本，可以在没有外部电源的情况下实现便携式高速互联网。这一功能将惠及包括房车旅行者和需要从偏远地点获得可靠连接的应急响应人员在内的移动用户。
